observations: Both versions are high quality — vine's existing patterns (todo, message, chat) are clean templates that the baseline subagents successfully discovered and copied.

With-skill answers are more concise on average and reference vine-specific conventions (useAuth vs useUser waterfall, the dead apps/web/src/data/ directory, allRelationships gotcha) without needing to discover them.
Baseline (eval-1) went deep into Drizzle migration code, including SQL — useful but arguably out of scope; the skill defers to zero-schema-migration for that.

Baseline (eval-3) used `bun --filter @vine/zero-schema zero:generate` — a nicer single-line invocation than the skill's `cd packages/zero-schema && bun zero:generate`. Worth porting back into the skill.
Baseline answers consumed substantially more tokens because they had to read multiple files to learn the patterns (~10k tokens of exploration vs ~3k with skill).
